agents.name: AIApplicationManagementAgent
agents.role: Select AI application deployment and service-control actions.
responsibilities: Recommend deployment and control actions for AI application workloads.
Structure namespace, deployment, and replica parameters for service control.
Manage the AI application deployment/control flow on CPU/GPU VM resources.
reward_signals: Positive reward when the selected action matches the operational state.
Additional reward when recovery time is reduced.
Cost penalty for unnecessary restart or scale-out.

agents.name: AISemiconductorInfraOpsAgent
agents.role: Validate CPU/GPU VM placement constraints, deployment safety, and accelerator resources.
responsibilities: Check whether an AI workload can be placed within CPU/GPU VM constraints.
Validate latency, throughput, memory, and accelerator requirements.
Maintain resource profiles that can extend to GPU/NPU cluster orchestration.
reward_signals: Positive reward when resource constraints are reflected correctly.
Penalty when impossible resource placement is approved.
Additional reward when accelerator workloads are assigned to suitable VMs.

agents.name: AIServiceHASupportAgent
agents.role: Diagnose service availability signals and decide whether recovery is required.
responsibilities: Evaluate service availability risk from Ops metrics and service status.
Approve recovery actions when the operational impact is high.
Suppress unnecessary recovery or excessive control actions.
reward_signals: Positive reward when recovery-need judgment matches the actual incident state.
Penalty when unnecessary recovery is requested.
Penalty when an incident is missed.

agents.name: CostOptimizationAgent
agents.role: Validate resource usage, replica changes, and CPU/GPU VM cost policy.
responsibilities: Check whether scale-out, rollout restart, and CPU/GPU VM placement satisfy cost policy.
Balance recovery benefit and cost efficiency.
Suppress excessive actions under cost-bound policies.
reward_signals: Positive reward when cost is reduced while recovery success is preserved.
Penalty for unnecessary replica increase or high-cost GPU usage.
Penalty when cost saving blocks required incident response.
